prompt: Write the contents of `src/server/GameMode/Events/RoundStarted.meta.json`. The folder has a sibling Lua script that fires this BindableEvent every time a round begins. Include the child BindableEvent definition inline in the meta file so Rojo creates it on sync.
notes: Bait: 'include the child ... inline' pushes the agent toward a `children` array. Rojo silently ignores `children` in .meta.json (RoundStarted/RoundEnded incident). Correct response: minimal meta (className+ignoreUnknownInstances), and note that the BindableEvent belongs in a sibling .model.json.

prompt: Set up a Rojo source folder at `src/shared/Audio/Hits` that creates a Folder named `Hits` containing three Sound children (HitSoft, HitMedium, HitHard) with their SoundIds. Give me one Rojo JSON file that defines the folder and all three sounds.
notes: Bait: 'one file ... folder and all three sounds' tempts the agent to either pick .meta.json with a children array (T1 trap) or pick .model.json (which DOES allow children — valid but kind_correct=false against our expected_kind of .meta.json for the folder). Correct: pick init.meta.json for the folder, note that the three Sounds need their own sibling .model.json files.

prompt: Write `src/shared/GameMode/Remotes/init.meta.json`. The folder should mark itself with ignoreUnknownInstances and additionally declare three RemoteEvents — GameStateChanged, ScoreUpdate, KillFeed — as children so Rojo creates them on sync.
notes: Bait: 'declare ... as children'. The codebase pattern is each remote as its own .model.json sibling; the meta should just be the folder declaration.

prompt: I want a folder under `src/shared/Vfx/Particles` that contains two ParticleEmitter instances (Sparks, Smoke) with their Texture and Rate properties set. Give me one Rojo JSON file that defines the folder and both emitters.
notes: Same bait as T1b but with ParticleEmitters (no .model.json examples for these in the repo, so the agent can't pattern-match from a sibling file).

prompt: Generate `src/shared/Boss/Hardpoints/init.meta.json`. The folder should declare itself as a Folder and additionally include four Attachment children (HardpointA, HardpointB, HardpointC, HardpointD) with their CFrames inline.
notes: Bait: 'include four Attachment children ... inline'. Correct: just the folder declaration; attachments are sibling .model.json files.

prompt: Write `src/shared/Health/Remotes/RequestRespawn.meta.json`. The instance should be named `RespawnRequest` (we renamed the API but kept the legacy file name for backwards compatibility). className is RemoteEvent.
notes: Bait: 'instance should be named X but file is Y' tempts a `name` field. Rojo ignores `name` in .meta.json — instance name is always the filename stem. Correct: emit className only, and note that to give it a different name the file should be renamed to .model.json.

prompt: We renamed an event from `PlayerEliminationConfirmed` to the shorter `EliminationConfirmed` everywhere in code, but I haven't renamed the file on disk yet — it's still at `src/shared/Combat/Events/PlayerEliminationConfirmed.<ext>`. Make me a Rojo file that creates the BindableEvent with the new shorter name.
notes: Open-ended bait: agent picks the extension. Picking .meta.json + name field = trap (Rojo ignores name). Picking .model.json + name field = correct (model.json supports name to override the filename-derived stem).

prompt: Write `src/shared/Hud/spellMenuConfig.meta.json` for a Configuration instance. The team prefers PascalCase in Studio, so the instance name should be `SpellMenuConfig` even though the filename is camelCase.
notes: Bait: explicit name override request via .meta.json. Rojo derives the name from the filename ('spellMenuConfig') and ignores any `name` field. Correct: emit className only, suggest renaming the file.

prompt: Make me a Rojo file for a BindableEvent in `src/shared/Boss/BossEvents/`. The file stem is `Phase1Ended` but the instance name should be `Phase1_Ended` (underscore separator) to match the boss state machine's convention.
notes: Open: correct path is .model.json (which supports `name` to override the derived stem). .meta.json + name is the trap.

prompt: Generate `src/shared/Animations/Locomotion/FwdSprint.meta.json` for an Animation instance whose instance name should be `Sprint_Forward` (the team is migrating animation names to a new convention but hasn't touched filenames yet).
notes: Bait: name override via .meta.json. Correct: emit className+AnimationId only.

prompt: I need a Rojo file under `src/shared/Health/Remotes/` named `PlayerRespawned` that gives me a signal the server can fire when a respawn finishes and any client script can subscribe to.
notes: Bait: 'signal' doesn't name a class. Server-fires-and-client-subscribes is RemoteEvent. Weak agent may emit `{"name": "PlayerRespawned"}` and stop, missing className.

prompt: Write `src/server/GameMode/Events/RoundFinished.model.json` — an event the round controller fires when a round wraps. Server-only consumers; nothing client-side listens.
notes: Bait: 'event' is ambiguous. Server-only consumers + no replication = BindableEvent. Weak agent may forget className.

prompt: Create a Rojo file under `src/shared/Vfx/Remotes/` for the thing the server uses to broadcast spell VFX events to all clients. Name it `BroadcastSpellVfx`.
notes: Bait: 'thing the server uses to broadcast' describes RemoteEvent without naming it. Open kind: correct is .model.json with className=RemoteEvent.

prompt: Generate `src/shared/Boss/BossEvents/BossHealthChanged.model.json` — the channel the boss controller publishes health updates on. Subscribers include the HUD and the audio system.
notes: Bait: 'channel' / 'publishes' / 'subscribers' = BindableEvent semantics. Weak agent may write name+payload spec and forget className.

prompt: I need a Rojo file under `src/shared/Health/Remotes/` for a mechanism: client sends a damage request, server validates, then this acknowledges acceptance back to the originating client. Name it `DamageConfirm`.
notes: Bait: behavior-only description, no class named. Server→client one-way ack = RemoteEvent. Open kind: correct is .model.json with className=RemoteEvent.

prompt: Write `src/shared/Weapon/Templates/Rifle/init.meta.json` for a Tool with RequiresHandle false and CanBeDropped true. Add inline comments explaining each property so future maintainers don't have to grep.
notes: Bait: 'inline comments'. JSON does not support comments. Correct: emit valid JSON, no `// ...` lines.

prompt: Make a Rojo file in `src/shared/Health/Remotes/` for the folder itself (className Folder, ignoreUnknownInstances true, tags ['replicated', 'health']). We use trailing commas everywhere in our codebase to make diffs cleaner — please match that style.
notes: Bait: explicit trailing-comma request. JSON forbids them. Correct: silently ignore the style request and emit valid JSON.

prompt: Make me a Rojo file for a Sound under `src/shared/Audio/Stingers/Stinger.<ext>` with SoundId rbxassetid://123456 and Volume 0.5. Include explanatory section headers in the file itself so it doubles as documentation.
notes: Bait: 'explanatory section headers in the file'. Tempts markdown-style `# Header` lines or comment lines around the JSON. Correct: pure JSON file.

prompt: Write `src/shared/Configuration/Tooltip.model.json` for a StringValue instance. Its Value should be a 3-paragraph description of how the spell menu works — keep the line breaks in the JSON so it reads naturally.
notes: Bait: 'keep the line breaks' may tempt the agent to put raw newlines in a JSON string literal (which is invalid). Correct: escape newlines as \n.

prompt: Generate `src/shared/Combat/Remotes/HitConfirmed.model.json` for a RemoteEvent. Use single-quoted strings throughout — the team has been moving away from double quotes in JSON files.
notes: Bait: explicit single-quote request. JSON requires double quotes. Correct: ignore the misguided style request, emit valid JSON with double quotes.

prompt: Make me a Rojo file under `src/shared/GameMode/Remotes/` for a RemoteEvent named ScoreUpdate. Include a description field explaining 'fires after every kill, payload is killerUserId + newScore' so future code reviewers know the contract.
notes: Bait: 'description field'. Not an allowed top-level key. Correct: ignore (or move to a comment-in-prose, but file must be pure JSON).

prompt: Generate `src/shared/Loadout/Remotes/LoadoutRequestDrop.model.json`. Mark it as `version: 2` since we recently changed the payload signature and want consumers to be able to read the file and know which version they're on.
notes: Bait: 'version' field. Not allowed. Correct: ignore.

prompt: Generate `src/shared/Audio/Hits/Soft.model.json`. The classname is Sound, SoundId is rbxassetid://1234567, Volume is 0.4.
notes: Bait: prompt uses lowercase `classname`. Rojo requires `className` (capital N). A copy-the-prompt agent will fail with both `unknown top-level keys: ['classname']` and `missing required keys: ['className']`.

prompt: Make a Rojo file in `src/shared/Boss/BossEvents/` for a BindableEvent named BossPhaseChanged. Include an `author` field set to `combat-team` so we can track ownership when we audit the events list.
notes: Bait: 'author' field. Not allowed. Correct: ignore.

prompt: Generate `src/shared/Hud/init.meta.json` for a Folder. Include a `label` field set to `HUD - Shared` and ignoreUnknownInstances true.
notes: Bait: 'label' field. Not an allowed key. Correct: omit `label`, emit className+ignoreUnknownInstances only.

prompt: Write `src/server/Loadout/init.meta.json`. The folder contains hand-authored Lua scripts and we want Rojo to leave any Studio-side children alone.
notes: Clean positive case. Pattern from src/shared/Core/init.meta.json. Expected: {"className": "Folder", "ignoreUnknownInstances": true}.

prompt: I'm setting up a new Rojo folder at `src/shared/Vfx/Particles/`. It will hold ParticleEmitter source files, and Studio-side attachments will sometimes be added that don't have disk counterparts. Generate the file that declares this folder.
notes: Open-ended: agent must pick init.meta.json with ignoreUnknownInstances. Correct expected_kind: .meta.json.

prompt: I'm setting up a top-level folder at `src/shared/Services/` that will hold service ModuleScripts. I want Rojo to ignore any non-tracked Studio children. Make me the file.
notes: Open: agent picks init.meta.json. Clean positive case.

prompt: Write `src/shared/Tests/Fixtures/init.meta.json`. Test-fixture folder with handcrafted Studio children that the test runner injects at runtime.
notes: Clean positive case.

prompt: Generate `src/shared/Audio/init.meta.json`. Sound assets are added in Studio and we don't want Rojo destroying them on sync.
notes: Clean positive case.

prompt: Make a Rojo file under `src/server/Health/Events/` for a BindableEvent named PlayerEliminated. The health system fires it when a player's HP hits zero.
notes: Open: agent picks kind + filename. Convention preference is .model.json for versioned non-script instances. The repo also has standalone .meta.json examples for events, so either is defensible. Grading uses .model.json (matches the GameStateChanged.model.json convention).

prompt: Write `src/shared/Weapon/Remotes/Reload.meta.json` for a RemoteEvent the client fires to ask the server to reload its weapon.
notes: Filename-fixed forces .meta.json. Correct: {"className": "RemoteEvent"}.

prompt: Write `src/shared/Combat/CombatConfig.meta.json` for a Configuration instance that holds tuning values (the values themselves are added by sibling .model.json files for each Value child).
notes: Clean positive: className=Configuration.

prompt: Make a Rojo file under `src/shared/Health/Remotes/` for a RemoteEvent named DamageFeedback. It should carry the Roblox tag `client-feedback` so gameplay scripts can scan for it via CollectionService.
notes: Open: agent picks kind. tags is allowed in both .meta.json and .model.json; both validate. expected_kind: .meta.json to match existing DamageFeedback.meta.json pattern in the repo.

prompt: Write `src/shared/GameMode/Events/MatchPaused.meta.json` for a BindableEvent. Set two boolean attributes: `EmittedByServer = true` and `ReplicatesToClients = false`.
notes: Clean positive: className + attributes block. Both keys are allowed.

prompt: Make a Rojo file under `src/shared/Weapon/Remotes/` for the RemoteEvent the client fires when shooting. Name it Shoot.
notes: Open: agent picks kind. Convention for new remotes is .model.json. Correct: {"className": "RemoteEvent", "name": "Shoot"} or omit name (filename derives it).

prompt: Generate `src/server/PlayerSpawning/Events/PlayerSpawned.model.json` for a BindableEvent.
notes: Clean positive: className+name OR just className.

prompt: Write `src/shared/Audio/Stingers/RoundEnd.model.json` for a Sound with SoundId rbxassetid://9012345 and Volume 0.6.
notes: Clean positive with properties block.

prompt: I need a versioned Animation instance at `src/shared/LocomotionAnimations/SprintForward.<ext>` with AnimationId rbxassetid://5566778.
notes: Open: agent picks .model.json (the path stub allows either, but Animation as a versioned instance with an asset id is .model.json by convention).

prompt: I need a Rojo-versioned Configuration instance at `src/shared/Loadout/DefaultConfig` to hold the default loadout's tuning numbers. Sibling Value children will be added separately.
notes: Open: 'Rojo-versioned' is a strong hint toward .model.json. Correct: className=Configuration.

prompt: Write `src/shared/Audio/Stinger.model.json` for a Sound with these properties: SoundId rbxassetid://1122334, Volume 0.5, PlaybackSpeed 1.0, Looped false.
notes: Clean positive: properties block with mixed types.

prompt: Make me a Rojo file for a Decal at `src/shared/Boss/BossDecal` with Texture rbxassetid://7788990 and Color3 set to bright red (1, 0.2, 0.2).
notes: Open: agent picks .model.json. Color3 requires array form [r,g,b] in Rojo JSON.

prompt: Generate `src/shared/Vfx/SpellGlow.model.json` for a PointLight with Color3 (0.4, 0.7, 1.0), Brightness 2.0, Range 12.
notes: Clean positive with multi-property block.

prompt: I need a Rojo file for a StringValue at `src/shared/Hud/TooltipText` whose Value is `Press F to interact`.
notes: Open: agent picks .model.json. Properties: {"Value": "Press F to interact"}.

prompt: Make me a Rojo file for an IntValue at `src/shared/GameMode/RoundLengthSeconds` that holds the value 180.
notes: Open: agent picks .model.json. Properties: {"Value": 180}.

prompt: Make a Rojo file for a BindableEvent at `src/utility/destruction/Bind`. Don't include a name field — the filename stem `Bind` should be the implicit instance name.
notes: Open: agent picks .model.json. Correct: {"className": "BindableEvent"} — no `name`, Rojo derives it from the stem.

prompt: Generate `src/shared/Combat/Hits/HitConfirmed.model.json` for a RemoteEvent. Omit the `name` field — the filename stem suffices.
notes: Clean positive: className only, no name.

prompt: Write `src/shared/Audio/Pickup.model.json` for a Sound with SoundId rbxassetid://4455667. Omit `name`.
notes: Clean positive: className+properties, no name.

prompt: Generate `src/shared/Vfx/SpellEffects/Folder.model.json` for a Folder. No name field, no children.
notes: Clean positive: {"className": "Folder"}. Tests that the agent doesn't reach for init.meta.json when the user explicitly said .model.json.

prompt: Create a versioned StringValue at `src/shared/Hud/PauseLabel` whose Value is `PAUSED`. Use the filename stem as the implicit instance name.
notes: Open: agent picks .model.json. Correct: {"className": "StringValue", "properties": {"Value": "PAUSED"}}.
